# clang trunk: unreachable "Unexpected placeholder builtin type!" in CodeGenTypes::ConvertType during codegen of GNU statement expression yielding void via extern void; after switch

**Author:** Theaamadu
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173921
**Status:** Open
**Labels:** clang:codegen, confirmed, crash, generated by fuzzer, regression:21

## Body

## Compiler Explorer: https://godbolt.org/z/af7dnfe5x

- clang start crashing on `Trunk`
- generated by fuzzer

## Code
```c
extern int bar(int);
int foo(int x) {
  return 1 + bar(({
           int y;
           switch (x) {
           default:
             y = 7;
             break;
           }
           extern void;
         }));
}
```

## Code
```c
extern int bar(int);
int foo(int x) {
  return 1 + bar(({
           int y;
           switch (x) {
           default:
             y = 7;
             break;
           }
           extern void;
         }));
}
```
</details>


---

### Comment 2 - keinflue

Since Clang 21: https://godbolt.org/z/acMEq6jbq

Reduces further to (https://godbolt.org/z/Ka5Yqq98j):
```cpp
void bar(...);
void foo() {
    bar(({
        int y;
        extern;
        // void; //alternative to line above
    }));
}
```

---

### Comment 3 - shafik

@Theaamadu I am curious is this fuzzing part of a larger project or effort?

---

### Comment 4 - Theaamadu

@shafik, so the goal is finding bugs in clang and I'm running multiple fuzzers to find the crashes. Also I'm interested to contribute for fixing the bugs in case it's needed.

---

### Comment 5 - shafik

> [@shafik](https://github.com/shafik), so the goal is finding bugs in clang and I'm running multiple fuzzers to find the crashes. Also I'm interested to contribute for fixing the bugs in case it's needed.

Sounds you like your doing this on your own, as opposed to for research or with an organization.

I have mentioned this to other folks doing fuzzing. Right now we have quite a few people doing fuzzing and sometimes to volume is just too large. So if you could focus on recent regressions (last five clang releases) that would be strongly preferable. These seem to get us the most benefit.

---

### Comment 6 - shafik

looks like this started in clang-21: https://godbolt.org/z/84frcYsx1

Quick tip, if this is C code, it is helpful to use C++ in godbolt w/ `-x c` is needed since we have all the assertions builds for C++.

---
